alkylation reagent: alkylated with 50 mM iodoacetamide (IAM) at room temperature for 30 min in the dark.
alkylation concentration: alkylated with 50 mM iodoacetamide (IAM) at room temperature for 30 min in the dark.
cleavage agent: in-solution digestion was then performed using trypsin at a 1:50 enzyme-to-protein (wt/wt) ratio at 37 °C overnight.
collision energy: with a normalized collision energy of 30%.
fragmentation method: the higher energy collision dissociation (HCD) was used for fragmentation.
instrument: LC-MS/MS analysis on a Thermo Orbitrap Fusion Lumos MS instrument.
labeling: the overall number of acetylated proteins and lysine residues in the mouse heart.
reduction reagent: 200 μL of 25 mM dithiothreitol (DTT) solution was used to reduce disulfides.
reduction concentration: 200 μL of 25 mM dithiothreitol (DTT) solution was used to reduce disulfides.
